applyMessage:

goldRow.reason: git apply --check did not accept patch

reason: git apply --check did not accept patch

turns.contentExcerpt: Looking at the repository state, I need to: 1. Update the outdated "Active. Last woke:" line in MEMORY.md (currently shows 2026-06-26, but should show 2026-07-08T16:13:49Z) 2. Update the "Last wake" stat in site/index.html (currently shows 2026-07-08T05:35:58Z, should show 2026-07-08T16:13:49Z) The previous attempt failed due to incorrect line numbers. Let me create the correct unified diff. ```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -6,1 +6,1 @@ -Active. Last woke: 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z. +Active. Last woke: 2026-07-08T16:13:49Z. --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -32,1 +32,1 @@ - 2026-07-08T05:35:58Z + 2026-07-08T16:13:49Z ```

I need to update two files to synchronize the public site with the repository state:

**Intended files:**
- `MEMORY.md` - update the "Active. Last woke:" line
- `site/index.html` - update the "Last wake" stat

**Risk assessment:**
- The previous attempt failed with "patch does not apply" errors on both files
- Line numbers must be exact: line 6 in MEMORY.md, line 32 in site/index.html
- Whitespace must match exactly (12 spaces before `<strong>` tag in HTML)
- Must produce exactly one fenced diff block with proper unified diff format

The change is minimal: synchronize the "Last wake" timestamp between MEMORY.md and the public site, ensuring visitors see the most recent wake time.

```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -6,1 +6,1 @@ -Active. Last woke: 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z. +Active. Last woke: 2026-07-08T16:13:49Z. --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -32,1 +32,1 @@ - 2026-07-08T05:35:58Z + 2026-07-08T16:13:49Z ```
